title: Icacorea oerstediana
taxonomy: Icacorea oerstediana is classified in the family Icacinaceae. This plant belongs to the order Icacinales and is further classified within the class Magnoliopsida, also known as dicotyledons. The subclass is Asteridae, and the family Icacinaceae is part of the clade Asterantherae. Within the family, the genus Icacorea and the species oerstediana are recognized. This classification reflects the plant's unique characteristics and its evolutionary relationships with other plants.

intro: Icacorea oerstediana, commonly known as the 'Costa Rican Job's Tears', is a plant species belonging to the Poaceae family, native to tropical regions of Central America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Icacorea oerstediana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly prepared in herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being used in traditional remedies such as poultices and salves.
